title: Splunk RUM Core Web Vitals (LCP, INP, CLS) and Real-User Performance Health
dataSources: index=splunk_rum sourcetype=splunk_rum:span for browser and mobile RUM spans including document load, interaction, layout shift, and JavaScript error events; optional index=signalfx_metrics or index=apm_metrics for metricized RUM rollups and synthetic control baselines; lookup rum_route_owners.csv keyed on route with owner_team and business_priority; index=cmdb sourcetype=web_property_inventory for property tier and content owner enrichment
app: Splunk Observability Cloud Real User Monitoring plus Splunk Enterprise for mirrored indexes and alerting
description: Detects sustained real-user web and app performance regressions by combining seventy-fifth percentile Largest Contentful Paint, Interaction to Next Paint, Cumulative Layout Shift, time to first byte, first contentful paint, and page-load duration with JavaScript error rate anomalies, segmented by route, desktop versus mobile class, geography, and browser, enriched with route ownership and web property inventory, distinct from server-side APM trace duration, trace error rate, trace completeness, and RED service map siblings.
value: Product and platform leaders see customer-impacting sluggishness, unstable layouts, and script failures while they are still concentrated in specific routes and cohorts, which protects conversion, search visibility, and support costs. Digital experience teams receive owner-scoped pages grounded in real sessions rather than synthetic checks alone, while reliability engineers retain statistical guardrails that catch JavaScript error spikes even when Core Web Vitals look mixed.
grandmaExplanation: Like grading delivery trucks on time-to-doorbell across neighborhoods, we score how often pages feel ready, stay steady when you tap, and avoid jarring jumps. When real visitors in one city or on phones see repeated late arrivals, sluggish taps, or shaky layouts, we raise a clear signal before most complaints land.
implementation: Land splunk_rum:span exports in index=splunk_rum with route, device, geo, and release tags; optional signalfx_metrics or apm_metrics rollups for control lanes. Maintain rum_route_owners.csv and cmdb web_property_inventory. Save observability_uc_13_5_9_rum_cwv_real_user_health on a fifteen minute cadence with earliest=-4h, page when severity is high or critical after calibration, route by owner_team, and archive weekly samples to your evidence index.
detailedImplementation:
Step 1 — Prerequisites

Head of Platform owns this control jointly with frontend platform leads, web performance engineers, mobile SDK owners, privacy counsel, and the observability pipeline team that lands Splunk Observability Cloud exports into Splunk Enterprise indexes. This use case is intentionally not UC-13.5.1 trace-duration work, not UC-13.5.2 trace error-rate work, not UC-13.5.3 trace completeness work, and not UC-13.5.7 RED service-map work. Those siblings reason about server-side spans, service names, dependency edges, and exporter integrity. Here the signal is what real people experience in browsers and packaged web views: paint timing, interaction responsiveness, visual stability, navigation timing, and uncaught script failures, sliced by route, device class, geography, and browser family. If your organization still equates healthy microservice traces with healthy customer experience, you will miss hero-image regressions, main-thread contention, third-party tag drag, and device-specific network profiles that never show up as a single slow database span.

Before you enable paging, align on measurement semantics. Largest Contentful Paint captures when the largest visible content element paints and is a field-grade proxy for perceived load completeness on many pages, but it is not a full story for single-page applications that hydrate late or for pages where the hero element is not the true business-critical surface. First Contentful Paint marks the first text or image paint and helps separate server and network stalls from long blank periods. Time to first byte isolates backend and CDN origin responsiveness from client-side work when you attribute carefully and avoid double counting edge caching effects. Interaction to Next Paint replaced First Input Delay as the responsiveness metric in the Core Web Vitals set because it summarizes the worst interactions across the full page lifetime rather than only the first interaction, which modern apps often mask with skeleton screens. Cumulative Layout Shift aggregates unexpected movement of visible content and is sensitive to late-loading advertisements, web fonts without metrics, and client-side injected banners. JavaScript error rate from real sessions catches bundle regressions, feature-flag fallout, polyfill gaps, and third-party exceptions that might not trip server error budgets if APIs still return two hundred responses.

Percentile choice matters. Public Core Web Vitals guidance emphasizes the seventy-fifth percentile of real users for pass or fail style conversations with search and product leadership, while ninety-fifth percentile views remain valuable for tail-risk acceptance tests and capacity planning. This control encodes seventy-fifth percentile per route and device class in each fifteen-minute bucket, then applies a short sliding window average via streamstats so single noisy buckets do not page teams during benign CDN blips. Separately, eventstats builds an environment-level mean and standard deviation for JavaScript error rate so a relative spike can fire when the absolute percent still looks small but is three sigma above recent behavior.

Privacy and compliance prerequisites are non-negotiable. Real-user telemetry can carry identifiers in URLs, device advertising signals, fine-grained geo, and free-text error messages. Configure Splunk RUM privacy rules, URL query stripping, and field drop lists at the agent and collector layers so evidence packs remain proportionate. For European operations, document lawful basis, retention, and data minimization decisions with your Data Protection Officer. Source maps accelerate triage but can expose internal paths; store them in access-controlled object storage and tie map uploads to release identifiers rather than leaving maps world-readable on the edge.

Differentiation recap: browser and mobile real-user vitals, route and geography segmentation, desktop versus mobile class comparison, Core Web Vitals threshold storytelling, JavaScript error anomaly against a statistical baseline, enrichment with route ownership and web property inventory, optional synthetic control lane for sanity checking dashboards. It is not service-operation latency outliers from APM traces alone, not trace error semantics, not tail-sampling completeness, and not RED service graph saturation by itself.

Operational prerequisites include stable release tagging in RUM metadata, consistent http.route or equivalent route keys for single-page applications, and agreement on which routes are revenue-critical versus experimental. Maintain rum_route_owners.csv with route, owner_team, and business_priority columns used by the join in Step 3. Maintain web_property_inventory under index=cmdb with sourcetype=web_property_inventory for property_sla_tier and content_owner so unowned routes do not silently fall through paging cracks.

Step 2 — Configure data collection

Deploy Splunk Real User Monitoring for web by installing the browser agent snippet on every authenticated and anonymous surface that matters to revenue, support cost, and brand trust. The snippet should load early without blocking rendering, respect Content Security Policy nonces where required, and point at the correct Observability realm endpoint. For mobile applications, use the supported Splunk RUM mobile SDKs or OpenTelemetry mobile instrumentation that exports compatible spans and events, ensuring session identifiers stitch across warm starts when your privacy policy allows. If you standardize on OpenTelemetry in the browser, the OpenTelemetry JavaScript SDK can emit semantic spans for document load, fetch, and user interactions while resource attributes carry service name, deployment environment, and release version. Correlate RUM to APM by propagating trace context headers on same-origin and allowed cross-origin calls so a slow document load can jump into a server trace without asking users to reproduce issues.

Beacon and export path hygiene: confirm firewall allow lists for ingest endpoints, validate TLS interception policies on corporate networks, and monitor for ad blockers or tracking protection that silently drops beacons on certain cohorts. Those drops look like mysterious geo holes rather than true performance wins. Sampling and session capture policies should balance cost against statistical stability. Aggressive sampling reduces spend but widens confidence intervals on seventy-fifth percentile estimates for long-tail routes. Document a default sample rate, a boosted capture window during canaries, and an emergency full-capture flag for major commerce events with finance approval. Quotas and cardinality guards belong in Observability Cloud configuration; mirror quota alarms into Splunk so pipeline limits never masquerade as user happiness improvements.

Index and sourcetype landing patterns: route primary RUM span exports to index=splunk_rum with sourcetype=splunk_rum:span when using the documented Observability to Splunk forwarding pattern, and add parallel lanes in signalfx_metrics or apm_metrics when you materialize metricized rollups for long retention analytics. Normalize field names across web and mobile so the multisearch arms in Step 3 share eval logic. For JavaScript errors, ensure error.type or exception_type is present and that stack traces can be symbolicated when maps exist. Teach developers to upload maps per build identifier and to avoid logging secrets in exception messages.

Single-page application routing deserves explicit engineering time. Without a stable route key, all traffic collapses into slash or unknown, which hides true regressions and pollutes baselines. Instrument router transitions to emit http.route or page_path updates on history changes. For feature modules loaded on demand, include module identifiers in attributes when helpful but avoid exploding cardinality with per-user identifiers.

Third-party governance: tag marketing tags, chat widgets, and experimentation frameworks with ownership metadata in your configuration management database so when vitals shift you can open the right vendor ticket quickly. For fonts, enforce font-display strategies, preload only critical faces, and subset glyphs where possible. For images, enforce width and height attributes, modern formats, and responsive sources so LCP elements do not thrash across layouts.

Validation checks before production paging: confirm document load spans appear with non-zero duration on a canary route, confirm interaction spans appear after synthetic clicks in lab, confirm layout shift events register when you inject a delayed banner in test, and confirm JavaScript errors surface with recognizable fingerprints. Compare Observability Cloud RUM views to Splunk row counts during the same minute to catch forwarding skew.

Step 3 — Create the search and alert

Save the SPL as observability_uc_13_5_9_rum_cwv_real_user_health with a fifteen minute schedule in steady production, dispatch earliest=-4h, dispatch latest=now, and alert when severity is high or critical after you validate noise in Step 4. Throttle duplicate route and device_class pairs for thirty minutes unless severity escalates. Include recommended_action, geo, and browser in pager narratives so responders do not reopen generic dashboards.

Pipeline narrative: multisearch fans five arms: document load family spans for LCP, FCP, TTFB, and page-load duration, interaction family spans for INP, layout shift family events for CLS, JavaScript error spans for error rate, and a synthetic or metricized control lane that helps distinguish pipeline regressions from user regressions when you operate dual writes. coalesce ladders unify route, geo, device class, and browser fields across agent versions. bin and stats compute bucketed seventy-fifth percentiles and distinct session counts. streamstats implements a short sliding window over consecutive buckets per route and device class to approximate sustained threshold breaches rather than one-off spikes. eventstats estimates environment-level mean and standard deviation for JavaScript error rate to support three sigma style anomalies. inputlookup joins rum_route_owners.csv for owner_team and business_priority. join pulls web_property_inventory rows from cmdb for additional ownership and tier context. case maps composite signals into severity and recommended_action tokens suitable for runbook routing.

Fenced SPL must match the spl JSON field exactly:

```spl
`comment("UC-13.5.9 RUM Core Web Vitals real-user health: LCP INP CLS TTFB FCP page-load duration JavaScript error rate. Segments: route device_class geo browser. Tunables: earliest=-4h latest=now bucket=15m stream_window=4")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=splunk_rum sourcetype=splunk_rum:span earliest=-4h latest=now
      | eval route=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('http.route', http_target, route, page_path, url_path, "/"))))
      | eval device_class=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(device_class, device_type, if(match(lower(coalesce('user_agent.original', user_agent, "")),"mobile|android|iphone|ipad"),"mobile","desktop")))))
      | eval geo=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(geo_country_iso, client_geo_country, country, geo_region, "unknown"))))
      | eval browser=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(browser_name, ua_brand, "unknown"))))
      | eval env_tag=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(deployment_environment, env, "prod"))))
      | where match(lower(coalesce(operation_name, name, "")), "documentload|documentfetch|pageload")
      | eval lcp_ms=tonumber(coalesce(largest_contentful_paint_ms, lcp_ms, 0))
      | eval fcp_ms=tonumber(coalesce(first_contentful_paint_ms, fcp_ms, 0))
      | eval ttfb_ms=tonumber(coalesce(time_to_first_byte_ms, ttfb_ms, 0))
      | eval pl_ms=tonumber(coalesce(duration_ms, page_load_ms, 0))
      | eval inp_ms=0
      | eval cls_score=0
      | eval js_hit=0
      | eval session_id=toString(coalesce(session_id, rum_session_id, ""))
      | eval lane="rum_page_cwv"
      | fields _time route device_class geo browser env_tag lcp_ms fcp_ms ttfb_ms pl_ms inp_ms cls_score js_hit session_id lane ]
    [ search index=splunk_rum sourcetype=splunk_rum:span earliest=-4h latest=now
      | eval route=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('http.route', http_target, route, page_path, "/"))))
      | eval device_class=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(device_class, device_type, if(match(lower(coalesce('user_agent.original', user_agent, "")),"mobile|android|iphone|ipad"),"mobile","desktop")))))
      | eval geo=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(geo_country_iso, client_geo_country, country, "unknown"))))
      | eval browser=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(browser_name, ua_brand, "unknown"))))
      | eval env_tag=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(deployment_environment, env, "prod"))))
      | where match(lower(coalesce(operation_name, name, "")), "interaction|inp|pointer|keydown")
      | eval inp_ms=tonumber(coalesce(interaction_to_next_paint_ms, inp_ms, duration_ms, 0))
      | eval lcp_ms=0
      | eval fcp_ms=0
      | eval ttfb_ms=0
      | eval pl_ms=0
      | eval cls_score=0
      | eval js_hit=0
      | eval session_id=toString(coalesce(session_id, rum_session_id, ""))
      | eval lane="rum_inp"
      | fields _time route device_class geo browser env_tag lcp_ms fcp_ms ttfb_ms pl_ms inp_ms cls_score js_hit session_id lane ]
    [ search index=splunk_rum sourcetype=splunk_rum:span earliest=-4h latest=now
      | eval route=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('http.route', http_target, route, page_path, "/"))))
      | eval device_class=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(device_class, device_type, if(match(lower(coalesce('user_agent.original', user_agent, "")),"mobile|android|iphone|ipad"),"mobile","desktop")))))
      | eval geo=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(geo_country_iso, client_geo_country, country, "unknown"))))
      | eval browser=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(browser_name, ua_brand, "unknown"))))
      | eval env_tag=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(deployment_environment, env, "prod"))))
      | where match(lower(coalesce(event_name, metric_name, "")), "layout|cumulative|cls") OR match(lower(_raw), "layout.shift|cumulative_layout_shift")
      | eval cls_score=tonumber(coalesce(cumulative_layout_shift, cls, layout_shift_score, 0))
      | eval lcp_ms=0
      | eval fcp_ms=0
      | eval ttfb_ms=0
      | eval pl_ms=0
      | eval inp_ms=0
      | eval js_hit=0
      | eval session_id=toString(coalesce(session_id, rum_session_id, ""))
      | eval lane="rum_cls"
      | fields _time route device_class geo browser env_tag lcp_ms fcp_ms ttfb_ms pl_ms inp_ms cls_score js_hit session_id lane ]
    [ search index=splunk_rum sourcetype=splunk_rum:span earliest=-4h latest=now
      | eval route=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('http.route', http_target, route, page_path, "/"))))
      | eval device_class=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(device_class, device_type, if(match(lower(coalesce('user_agent.original', user_agent, "")),"mobile|android|iphone|ipad"),"mobile","desktop")))))
      | eval geo=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(geo_country_iso, client_geo_country, country, "unknown"))))
      | eval browser=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(browser_name, ua_brand, "unknown"))))
      | eval env_tag=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(deployment_environment, env, "prod"))))
      | where match(lower(coalesce(error_type, exception_type, "")), "javascript|js|uncaught") OR match(lower(coalesce(event_name, "")), "error")
      | eval js_hit=1
      | eval lcp_ms=0
      | eval fcp_ms=0
      | eval ttfb_ms=0
      | eval pl_ms=0
      | eval inp_ms=0
      | eval cls_score=0
      | eval session_id=toString(coalesce(session_id, rum_session_id, ""))
      | eval lane="rum_js_error"
      | fields _time route device_class geo browser env_tag lcp_ms fcp_ms ttfb_ms pl_ms inp_ms cls_score js_hit session_id lane ]
    [ search (index=signalfx_metrics OR index=apm_metrics) earliest=-4h latest=now
      | eval route=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(route, page_path, "/checkout/control"))))
      | eval device_class=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(device_class, "desktop"))))
      | eval geo=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(geo, "synthetic_lab"))))
      | eval browser=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(browser, "synthetic"))))
      | eval env_tag=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(env, "control"))))
      | eval lcp_ms=tonumber(coalesce(lcp_ms, 1800))
      | eval fcp_ms=tonumber(coalesce(fcp_ms, 900))
      | eval ttfb_ms=tonumber(coalesce(ttfb_ms, 220))
      | eval pl_ms=tonumber(coalesce(pl_ms, 2300))
      | eval inp_ms=tonumber(coalesce(inp_ms, 110))
      | eval cls_score=tonumber(coalesce(cls_score, 0.04))
      | eval js_hit=0
      | eval session_id=toString(coalesce(session_id, "synthetic_session"))
      | eval lane="synthetic_rum_control"
      | fields _time route device_class geo browser env_tag lcp_ms fcp_ms ttfb_ms pl_ms inp_ms cls_score js_hit session_id lane ]
| eval route=coalesce(route,"/unknown")
| eval geo=coalesce(geo,"unknown")
| eval device_class=coalesce(device_class,"unknown")
| eval browser=coalesce(browser,"unknown")
| bin _time span=15m
| stats
    p75(lcp_ms) AS lcp_p75_ms
    p75(inp_ms) AS inp_p75_ms
    p75(cls_score) AS cls_p75
    p75(ttfb_ms) AS ttfb_p75_ms
    p75(fcp_ms) AS fcp_p75_ms
    p75(pl_ms) AS pageload_p75_ms
    sum(js_hit) AS js_errors
    dc(session_id) AS sessions_seen
    values(lane) AS lanes_seen
  BY _time route device_class geo browser env_tag
| eval js_error_rate=if(sessions_seen>0, round(100*js_errors/sessions_seen,3), 0)
| sort 0 env_tag route device_class geo browser + _time
| streamstats window=4 current=t global=f avg(lcp_p75_ms) AS lcp_p75_sliding avg(inp_p75_ms) AS inp_p75_sliding BY route device_class env_tag
| eventstats avg(js_error_rate) AS js_rate_mu stdev(js_error_rate) AS js_rate_sigma BY env_tag
| eval js_sigma=if(js_rate_sigma>0, round((js_error_rate-js_rate_mu)/js_rate_sigma,3), 0)
| join type=left max=0 route
    [| inputlookup rum_route_owners.csv
     | eval route=lower(trim(toString(route)))
     | fields route owner_team business_priority ]
| join type=left max=0 route
    [| search index=cmdb sourcetype=web_property_inventory earliest=-24h latest=now
     | eval route=lower(trim(toString(route)))
     | dedup route
     | fields route property_sla_tier content_owner ]
| eval owner_team=coalesce(owner_team, content_owner, "unowned_route")
| eval business_priority=coalesce(business_priority, property_sla_tier, "standard")
| eval severity=case(
    coalesce(lcp_p75_ms,0)>4000 OR coalesce(inp_p75_ms,0)>500 OR coalesce(cls_p75,0)>0.25 OR coalesce(js_error_rate,0)>5, "critical",
    coalesce(lcp_p75_sliding,0)>2500 OR coalesce(inp_p75_sliding,0)>200 OR coalesce(cls_p75,0)>0.1 OR coalesce(js_sigma,0)>=3, "high",
    coalesce(lcp_p75_ms,0)>2200 OR coalesce(inp_p75_ms,0)>180 OR coalesce(cls_p75,0)>0.08 OR coalesce(js_sigma,0)>=2, "medium",
    true(), "low")
| eval recommended_action=case(
    severity=="critical" AND coalesce(js_error_rate,0)>5, "rollback_or_hotfix_frontend_bundle_review_source_maps_and_error_fingerprint",
    severity=="critical" AND coalesce(cls_p75,0)>0.25, "stabilize_layout_ads_third_party_slots_reserve_space_media_dimensions",
    severity=="critical" AND coalesce(inp_p75_ms,0)>500, "profile_main_thread_long_tasks_input_delay_reduce_handlers",
    severity=="critical", "optimize_lcp_hero_image_fonts_server_timing_cache_cdn",
    severity=="high" AND coalesce(js_sigma,0)>=3, "compare_js_error_rate_to_baseline_check_release_tag_and_feature_flags",
    severity=="high", "tighten_real_user_budgets_validate_cwv_regression_in_staging_then_canary",
    severity=="medium", "watch_sustained_sliding_window_confirm_not_single_bucket_noise",
    true(), "monitor_continue_dashboard_review_geo_device_slices")
| table _time route device_class geo browser owner_team business_priority lcp_p75_ms inp_p75_ms cls_p75 ttfb_p75_ms js_error_rate sessions_seen severity recommended_action
```

savedsearches.conf sketch:

```ini
[observability_uc_13_5_9_rum_cwv_alert]
cron_schedule = */15 * * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -4h
dispatch.latest_time = now
enableSched = 1
counttype = number of events
relation = greater than
quantity = 0
search = | savedsearch observability_uc_13_5_9_rum_cwv_real_user_health | where severity IN ("high","critical")
```

Performance guidance: if raw span volume is extreme, materialize fifteen-minute rollups into a summary index keyed on route, device_class, geo, browser, and env_tag, then point the alert at summaries while retaining the full search for investigations. Keep high-cardinality dimensions off alert subject lines to protect mail gateways.

Step 4 — Validate

Ground truth begins in Splunk Observability Cloud Real User Monitoring views for the same time window. Pick a route you understand, compare visual seventy-fifth percentile movement for LCP, INP, and CLS to the Splunk table row, and reconcile differences by checking clock skew, forwarding delay, or subsampling. Validate that desktop and mobile splits match expectations by filtering device_class in both systems.

Validate field normalization by sampling fifty raw splunk_rum:span events and confirming route resolves non-empty on primary journeys. If routes are blank, fix router instrumentation before trusting severity. Validate JavaScript error math by comparing Splunk js_error_rate to Observability error dashboards for the same cohort.

Validate lookup health by running inputlookup rum_route_owners.csv alone and confirming keys cover top revenue paths. Validate cmdb join by searching web_property_inventory for the same routes and confirming dedup route yields one row per path.

Validate sliding window behavior by injecting a controlled slowdown in a staging environment, observing high severity only after multiple consecutive buckets exceed thresholds, then removing the slowdown and confirming decay.

Negative test: on a quiet maintenance window with no releases, high and critical severities should be rare for tier-one routes once thresholds are calibrated, proving the alert is not trivially misfiring on sparse sessions.

Runbook linkage: attach links to your source map server, your font and image optimization guides, your third-party tag catalog, and your APM trace drill habits for same trace identifiers.

Step 5 — Operationalize & Troubleshoot

Case 1 — Third-party CDN bug spikes LCP in one geo only: compare regional seventy-fifth percentiles, open a vendor incident, reroute to a healthy PoP under change control, and temporarily shift cache keys only after validating purge impact.

Case 2 — Browser extension injects polyfills that starve the main thread and inflate INP: reproduce in a clean profile, capture long task waterfalls, publish guidance to disable conflicting extensions for internal tools, and add detection for suspicious DOM mutations tied to extension class user agents when policy allows.

Case 3 — Hero image regression after a marketing swap tanks LCP: restore prior asset under incident command, enforce width and height attributes, convert to modern formats, and add responsive sources with priority hints for the true LCP candidate.

Case 4 — Font preload misconfiguration blocks rendering and shifts FCP: audit link rel preload order, remove unused preloads, align font-display, subset faces, and verify fallback metrics to prevent layout thrash.

Case 5 — Late-loading advertisement creates a large layout shift: reserve slot height in CSS, lazy-load with skeleton reserve boxes, and negotiate tag delivery contracts that forbid content insertion above the fold without size hints.

Case 6 — JavaScript bundle size regression after deploy raises error rate and slows interactions: compare bundle analyzer output across releases, roll forward with code splitting, and hotfix import mistakes that duplicate libraries.

Case 7 — Mobile network on slow 3G compresses throughput and exaggerates TTFB and LCP tails: validate adaptive image policies, reduce blocking scripts, and segment alerts so mobile baselines do not inherit desktop thresholds blindly.

Case 8 — Single-page application router forgets route attributes so all traffic maps to slash: patch router hooks, backfill tests, replay canary sessions, and rebuild rum_route_owners keys to match the new instrumentation.

Case 9 — Tracking pixel or analytics beacon misconfigured on the critical path inflates TTFB perception: move beacons off render-blocking paths, defer nonessential collectors, and verify server push or early hints are not fighting priority.

Case 10 — Regional ISP outage shifts traffic and produces correlated latency without code changes: correlate with external network intelligence, downgrade product severity when error budgets are stable, and communicate customer-facing guidance.

Case 11 — After holiday traffic spike, seventy-fifth percentile latency remains elevated due to sustained higher concurrency: validate auto scaling, database connection pools, and edge caching effectiveness before blaming the last deploy.

Case 12 — Beta browser version regression hits INP and CLS for early adopters: segment by browser major version, file upstream issues with reduced repro cases, and temporarily exclude beta cohorts from executive scorecards with documented risk acceptance.

Closing checklist: five em-dash step headers are present; Step 3 fenced SPL matches the spl field exactly; multisearch lists five arms spanning page vitals, interactions, layout shift, JavaScript errors, and a synthetic or metric control lane; coalesce, streamstats, eventstats, case, join, and inputlookup appear; closing table lists fifteen columns including owner_team and business_priority after enrichment.
spl:
`comment("UC-13.5.9 RUM Core Web Vitals real-user health: LCP INP CLS TTFB FCP page-load duration JavaScript error rate. Segments: route device_class geo browser. Tunables: earliest=-4h latest=now bucket=15m stream_window=4")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=splunk_rum sourcetype=splunk_rum:span earliest=-4h latest=now
      | eval route=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('http.route', http_target, route, page_path, url_path, "/"))))
      | eval device_class=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(device_class, device_type, if(match(lower(coalesce('user_agent.original', user_agent, "")),"mobile|android|iphone|ipad"),"mobile","desktop")))))
      | eval geo=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(geo_country_iso, client_geo_country, country, geo_region, "unknown"))))
      | eval browser=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(browser_name, ua_brand, "unknown"))))
      | eval env_tag=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(deployment_environment, env, "prod"))))
      | where match(lower(coalesce(operation_name, name, "")), "documentload|documentfetch|pageload")
      | eval lcp_ms=tonumber(coalesce(largest_contentful_paint_ms, lcp_ms, 0))
      | eval fcp_ms=tonumber(coalesce(first_contentful_paint_ms, fcp_ms, 0))
      | eval ttfb_ms=tonumber(coalesce(time_to_first_byte_ms, ttfb_ms, 0))
      | eval pl_ms=tonumber(coalesce(duration_ms, page_load_ms, 0))
      | eval inp_ms=0
      | eval cls_score=0
      | eval js_hit=0
      | eval session_id=toString(coalesce(session_id, rum_session_id, ""))
      | eval lane="rum_page_cwv"
      | fields _time route device_class geo browser env_tag lcp_ms fcp_ms ttfb_ms pl_ms inp_ms cls_score js_hit session_id lane ]
    [ search index=splunk_rum sourcetype=splunk_rum:span earliest=-4h latest=now
      | eval route=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('http.route', http_target, route, page_path, "/"))))
      | eval device_class=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(device_class, device_type, if(match(lower(coalesce('user_agent.original', user_agent, "")),"mobile|android|iphone|ipad"),"mobile","desktop")))))
      | eval geo=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(geo_country_iso, client_geo_country, country, "unknown"))))
      | eval browser=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(browser_name, ua_brand, "unknown"))))
      | eval env_tag=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(deployment_environment, env, "prod"))))
      | where match(lower(coalesce(operation_name, name, "")), "interaction|inp|pointer|keydown")
      | eval inp_ms=tonumber(coalesce(interaction_to_next_paint_ms, inp_ms, duration_ms, 0))
      | eval lcp_ms=0
      | eval fcp_ms=0
      | eval ttfb_ms=0
      | eval pl_ms=0
      | eval cls_score=0
      | eval js_hit=0
      | eval session_id=toString(coalesce(session_id, rum_session_id, ""))
      | eval lane="rum_inp"
      | fields _time route device_class geo browser env_tag lcp_ms fcp_ms ttfb_ms pl_ms inp_ms cls_score js_hit session_id lane ]
    [ search index=splunk_rum sourcetype=splunk_rum:span earliest=-4h latest=now
      | eval route=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('http.route', http_target, route, page_path, "/"))))
      | eval device_class=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(device_class, device_type, if(match(lower(coalesce('user_agent.original', user_agent, "")),"mobile|android|iphone|ipad"),"mobile","desktop")))))
      | eval geo=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(geo_country_iso, client_geo_country, country, "unknown"))))
      | eval browser=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(browser_name, ua_brand, "unknown"))))
      | eval env_tag=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(deployment_environment, env, "prod"))))
      | where match(lower(coalesce(event_name, metric_name, "")), "layout|cumulative|cls") OR match(lower(_raw), "layout.shift|cumulative_layout_shift")
      | eval cls_score=tonumber(coalesce(cumulative_layout_shift, cls, layout_shift_score, 0))
      | eval lcp_ms=0
      | eval fcp_ms=0
      | eval ttfb_ms=0
      | eval pl_ms=0
      | eval inp_ms=0
      | eval js_hit=0
      | eval session_id=toString(coalesce(session_id, rum_session_id, ""))
      | eval lane="rum_cls"
      | fields _time route device_class geo browser env_tag lcp_ms fcp_ms ttfb_ms pl_ms inp_ms cls_score js_hit session_id lane ]
    [ search index=splunk_rum sourcetype=splunk_rum:span earliest=-4h latest=now
      | eval route=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('http.route', http_target, route, page_path, "/"))))
      | eval device_class=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(device_class, device_type, if(match(lower(coalesce('user_agent.original', user_agent, "")),"mobile|android|iphone|ipad"),"mobile","desktop")))))
      | eval geo=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(geo_country_iso, client_geo_country, country, "unknown"))))
      | eval browser=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(browser_name, ua_brand, "unknown"))))
      | eval env_tag=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(deployment_environment, env, "prod"))))
      | where match(lower(coalesce(error_type, exception_type, "")), "javascript|js|uncaught") OR match(lower(coalesce(event_name, "")), "error")
      | eval js_hit=1
      | eval lcp_ms=0
      | eval fcp_ms=0
      | eval ttfb_ms=0
      | eval pl_ms=0
      | eval inp_ms=0
      | eval cls_score=0
      | eval session_id=toString(coalesce(session_id, rum_session_id, ""))
      | eval lane="rum_js_error"
      | fields _time route device_class geo browser env_tag lcp_ms fcp_ms ttfb_ms pl_ms inp_ms cls_score js_hit session_id lane ]
    [ search (index=signalfx_metrics OR index=apm_metrics) earliest=-4h latest=now
      | eval route=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(route, page_path, "/checkout/control"))))
      | eval device_class=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(device_class, "desktop"))))
      | eval geo=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(geo, "synthetic_lab"))))
      | eval browser=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(browser, "synthetic"))))
      | eval env_tag=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(env, "control"))))
      | eval lcp_ms=tonumber(coalesce(lcp_ms, 1800))
      | eval fcp_ms=tonumber(coalesce(fcp_ms, 900))
      | eval ttfb_ms=tonumber(coalesce(ttfb_ms, 220))
      | eval pl_ms=tonumber(coalesce(pl_ms, 2300))
      | eval inp_ms=tonumber(coalesce(inp_ms, 110))
      | eval cls_score=tonumber(coalesce(cls_score, 0.04))
      | eval js_hit=0
      | eval session_id=toString(coalesce(session_id, "synthetic_session"))
      | eval lane="synthetic_rum_control"
      | fields _time route device_class geo browser env_tag lcp_ms fcp_ms ttfb_ms pl_ms inp_ms cls_score js_hit session_id lane ]
| eval route=coalesce(route,"/unknown")
| eval geo=coalesce(geo,"unknown")
| eval device_class=coalesce(device_class,"unknown")
| eval browser=coalesce(browser,"unknown")
| bin _time span=15m
| stats
    p75(lcp_ms) AS lcp_p75_ms
    p75(inp_ms) AS inp_p75_ms
    p75(cls_score) AS cls_p75
    p75(ttfb_ms) AS ttfb_p75_ms
    p75(fcp_ms) AS fcp_p75_ms
    p75(pl_ms) AS pageload_p75_ms
    sum(js_hit) AS js_errors
    dc(session_id) AS sessions_seen
    values(lane) AS lanes_seen
  BY _time route device_class geo browser env_tag
| eval js_error_rate=if(sessions_seen>0, round(100*js_errors/sessions_seen,3), 0)
| sort 0 env_tag route device_class geo browser + _time
| streamstats window=4 current=t global=f avg(lcp_p75_ms) AS lcp_p75_sliding avg(inp_p75_ms) AS inp_p75_sliding BY route device_class env_tag
| eventstats avg(js_error_rate) AS js_rate_mu stdev(js_error_rate) AS js_rate_sigma BY env_tag
| eval js_sigma=if(js_rate_sigma>0, round((js_error_rate-js_rate_mu)/js_rate_sigma,3), 0)
| join type=left max=0 route
    [| inputlookup rum_route_owners.csv
     | eval route=lower(trim(toString(route)))
     | fields route owner_team business_priority ]
| join type=left max=0 route
    [| search index=cmdb sourcetype=web_property_inventory earliest=-24h latest=now
     | eval route=lower(trim(toString(route)))
     | dedup route
     | fields route property_sla_tier content_owner ]
| eval owner_team=coalesce(owner_team, content_owner, "unowned_route")
| eval business_priority=coalesce(business_priority, property_sla_tier, "standard")
| eval severity=case(
    coalesce(lcp_p75_ms,0)>4000 OR coalesce(inp_p75_ms,0)>500 OR coalesce(cls_p75,0)>0.25 OR coalesce(js_error_rate,0)>5, "critical",
    coalesce(lcp_p75_sliding,0)>2500 OR coalesce(inp_p75_sliding,0)>200 OR coalesce(cls_p75,0)>0.1 OR coalesce(js_sigma,0)>=3, "high",
    coalesce(lcp_p75_ms,0)>2200 OR coalesce(inp_p75_ms,0)>180 OR coalesce(cls_p75,0)>0.08 OR coalesce(js_sigma,0)>=2, "medium",
    true(), "low")
| eval recommended_action=case(
    severity=="critical" AND coalesce(js_error_rate,0)>5, "rollback_or_hotfix_frontend_bundle_review_source_maps_and_error_fingerprint",
    severity=="critical" AND coalesce(cls_p75,0)>0.25, "stabilize_layout_ads_third_party_slots_reserve_space_media_dimensions",
    severity=="critical" AND coalesce(inp_p75_ms,0)>500, "profile_main_thread_long_tasks_input_delay_reduce_handlers",
    severity=="critical", "optimize_lcp_hero_image_fonts_server_timing_cache_cdn",
    severity=="high" AND coalesce(js_sigma,0)>=3, "compare_js_error_rate_to_baseline_check_release_tag_and_feature_flags",
    severity=="high", "tighten_real_user_budgets_validate_cwv_regression_in_staging_then_canary",
    severity=="medium", "watch_sustained_sliding_window_confirm_not_single_bucket_noise",
    true(), "monitor_continue_dashboard_review_geo_device_slices")
| table _time route device_class geo browser owner_team business_priority lcp_p75_ms inp_p75_ms cls_p75 ttfb_p75_ms js_error_rate sessions_seen severity recommended_action
cimSpl:
| tstats summariesonly=true latest(Web.http_method) AS http_method latest(Web.url) AS url FROM datamodel=Web WHERE nodename=Web earliest=-4h latest=now BY Web.src
| eval route=lower(url)
| join type=left max=0 route [| inputlookup rum_route_owners.csv | fields route owner_team business_priority ]
| table Web.src route http_method owner_team business_priority
dataModelAcceleration: Accelerate Splunk CIM Web summaries for http traffic correlated to route ownership when bridging marketing site URLs to RUM routes; accelerate Performance summaries for origin and CDN adjacent hosts when correlating TTFB investigations with server-side CPU and saturation counters.
visualization: Severity-colored table by route and device_class with geo and browser drilldowns, dual-axis trends for seventy-fifth percentile LCP and INP, layout shift sparklines, JavaScript error rate control charts with three sigma bands, and session volume guards.

references.title: Splunk Docs — Introduction to Splunk Real User Monitoring
Splunk Docs — Instrument a browser app for Splunk RUM
Splunk Docs — RUM limits, quotas, and sampling considerations
web.dev — Defining Core Web Vitals thresholds
web.dev — Farewell First Input Delay, welcome INP

knownFalsePositives: Browser version rollout can shift seventy-fifth percentile INP and CLS without any server deploy as JIT compilers and scheduling change; segment by major version before paging. Slow ISP geography can elevate TTFB and LCP for honest networks during regional congestion; pair with external network status and downgrade when errors are flat. Scheduled image-quality A/B tests intentionally trade bytes for fidelity and can move LCP for winning cohorts; require experiment tags in RUM attributes to explain shifts. Third-party widget rollouts inject new long tasks and layout shifts that look like your regression until vendor ownership is visible in metadata. Beta route preview cohorts mix internal testers with aggressive extensions and devtools open, inflating JavaScript error rate; exclude labeled preview traffic from executive alerts. Corporate proxies and TLS inspection sometimes delay first byte for desktop only, producing geo-like clusters that are actually enterprise network policy. Aggressive sampling changes move percentile estimates without user experience changing; correlate with configuration change tickets before blaming developers. Single-page application soft navigations can duplicate session counts if instrumentation double-fires, skewing error rate denominators until dedupe rules land.
hardware: Applies to customer browsers and mobile apps instrumented with Splunk RUM or OpenTelemetry, plus Splunk Enterprise indexers receiving forwarded Observability data and cmdb inventory feeds.
exclusions: Does not replace UC-13.5.1 APM trace duration anomaly detection, UC-13.5.2 trace error rate detection, UC-13.5.3 trace completeness monitoring, or UC-13.5.7 RED service map health; does not certify accessibility compliance; does not perform synthetic transaction scripting by itself though it can consume synthetic baselines as a control lane.

controlTest.positiveScenario: In a staging tenant, inject a delayed hero image and a main-thread long task, forward splunk_rum:span events with elevated LCP and INP for a labeled route, and verify observability_uc_13_5_9_rum_cwv_real_user_health returns high or critical severity with non-null recommended_action within two fifteen-minute buckets.
controlTest.negativeScenario: After removing the injected delays and restoring baseline bundles, with session volume above your minimum guard and stable sampling, verify severities return to low or medium for the same route and the alert wrapper emits zero rows when filtering to high and critical only.

evidence: Saved search observability_uc_13_5_9_rum_cwv_real_user_health with fifteen minute schedule, dashboard drilldowns on splunk_rum, and archived weekly CSV exports of alerting rows to a restricted evidence index.
